stimulus: The fossil Diplomystus dentatus is most often recovered from the Green River Formation in Wyoming. Many of its specimens are preserved in such fine-grained limestone that even individual scales and fin rays appear sharp and undistorted to the naked eye. Specimens of the related fish Knightia eocaena from the same deposit are also remarkably well preserved; ______blank their scales and fin rays usually require a hand lens to be made out clearly.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "Even so" logically introduces a partial concession or exception: although Knightia specimens are also well preserved, their fine detail is only visible with a hand lens rather than to the naked eye, distinguishing them from Diplomystus. The transition signals that the upcoming claim qualifies the prior one.

Choice B is incorrect because "for instance" would frame the second clause as an example of high-quality preservation visible to the naked eye, but it instead notes a limitation. Choice C is incorrect because "accordingly" would signal cause and effect, yet needing a hand lens is not a result of good preservation. Choice D is incorrect because "likewise" would mark the two clauses as making the same point, but the second clause introduces a qualifying difference.
